name: Apples
meta: The 'Wash' is a lie. Pesticides are in the wax.
description: Apples are often coated in shellac or petroleum-based wax to preserve shelf life, trapping pesticides underneath. Conventional apples are often over a year old.

tidbits.label: Storage Life
tidbits.text: Conventional apples can be stored in cold rooms for 9-12 months before hitting shelves.

tidbits.label: Pesticide Load
tidbits.text: Ranked consistently in the top 5 of the Dirty Dozen.

name: Blueberries
description: Standard cultivated blueberries are heavily sprayed. Wild ones are nutrient powerhouses and naturally pesticide-free.

tidbits.label: Wild vs Cultivated
tidbits.text: Wild blueberries have 2x the antioxidant capacity and 33% more anthocyanins than cultivated.

tidbits.label: Pesticide Load
tidbits.text: Cultivated berries have a high skin-to-volume ratio, meaning more surface area for chemical residue.

name: Strawberries
meta: The most sprayed crop in America.
description: Strawberries lack a protective skin and are often sprayed right before harvest to prevent mold.

tidbits.label: Pesticide Cocktail
tidbits.text: A single sample can contain residues of 20+ different pesticides.

name: Avocados
meta: Save your money. Conventional is fine.
description: Avocados consistently top the 'Clean Fifteen' list because their thick skin protects the fruit.

tidbits.label: Pesticide Residue
tidbits.text: Fewer than 2% of conventional avocados show any detectable pesticides.

name: Oats / Oatmeal
description: Conventional oats are heavily sprayed with glyphosate as a drying agent. Even Organic oats often test positive due to soil drift.

tidbits.label: Glyphosate
tidbits.text: Used as a desiccant right before harvest. Most 'natural' oats are surprisingly high in this chemical.

tidbits.label: One Degree
tidbits.text: One Degree Organic Foods offers third-party 'Non-Glyphosate Certified' oats.

name: Snack / Energy Bars
description: Most bars contain oats, which contain glyphosate. Buying Organic bars significantly reduces your exposure.

tidbits.label: The Solution
tidbits.text: Look for brands that prioritize glyphosate-free sourcing.

name: Milk
description: Standard milk comes from cows that're fed the cheapest, genetically modified feed.

tidbits.label: A1 vs A2
tidbits.text: Many 'lactose intolerant' people are actually just reacting to the A1 protein in standard milk.
comes from heritage breeds and is easier to digest.

tidbits.label: Grass-Fed
tidbits.text: Grass-fed milk has a better Omega-3 to Omega-6 ratio.

name: Eggs
description: Cage-free is marketing fluff. The birds never see the sun. Pasture-raised get 108sq ft each.

tidbits.label: Nutrient Density
tidbits.text: Pasture eggs have 2x more Omega-3s and 3x more Vitamin D from bugs and sunlight.

tidbits.label: Gold Standard
tidbits.text: Look for 'Pasture-Raised + Certified Humane' logos.

name: Salmon
meta: Wild Alaskan salmon. 'Atlantic' is code for 'farmed'.
description: Wild Alaskan salmon is where it's at. Atlantic salmon is all farmed.

tidbits.label: Contaminants
tidbits.text: Farmed salmon can have 16x the PCBs of wild caught salmon. They're fed synthetic dyes to turn their flesh pink.

tidbits.label: idk
tidbits.text: idk.

name: Beef
description: Standard beef is 'Grain-Finished' in feedlots, which spikes inflammatory Omega-6 levels.

tidbits.label: Omega Ratio
tidbits.text: Grass-fed has an Omega 3:6 ratio of ~1:3. Grain-fed can be 1:15.

name: Chicken
description: Water-chilled chicken adds water weight and dilutes flavor. Air-chilled is cleaner.

tidbits.label: Water Weight
tidbits.text: Water-chilled chicken can be up to 5% chlorinated water.

name: Corn
meta: Conventional is safe, but will be GMO. Look for organic (which is sometimes easier found frozen).
description: Corn is protected by its husk.

tidbits.label: Pesticide Load
tidbits.text: Ranked as one of the cleanest items for pesticide residue.

name: Pineapples
meta: Save your money. Conventional is fine.
description: The thick, scaly skin of a pineapple provides excellent protection against pesticides.

tidbits.label: Residue testing
tidbits.text: One of the safest non-organic produce items you can buy.

name: Bananas
meta: Save your money. Conventional is fine.
description: Bananas are one of the safest fruits to buy conventional because the thick skin is discarded.

tidbits.label: Residue testing
tidbits.text: One of the safest non-organic produce items you can buy.

name: Onions
meta: Save your money. Conventional is fine.
description: Onions are naturally pest-resistant and require very few chemicals during growth.

tidbits.label: Pesticide Load
tidbits.text: Consistently shows the lowest pesticide residues of all produce.

name: Papaya
meta: Conventional, organic if you want non-GMO.
description: Most Hawaiian papayas are GMO, but pesticide residue is very low. Buy organic if you want non-GMO.

tidbits.label: Clean Score
tidbits.text: Over 80% of papayas tested had zero pesticide residue.

name: Sweet Peas (Frozen)
meta: Frozen is the meta for nutrient preservation.
description: Peas are flash-frozen at peak ripeness and show very low pesticide residue.

tidbits.label: Clean Score
tidbits.text: One of the top Clean Fifteen performers.

name: Asparagus
description: Asparagus grows quickly and has fewer natural pests, requiring few chemicals.

tidbits.label: Residue testing
tidbits.text: Shows very low levels of pesticide residue compared to other green vegetables.
